Football.title: Man Utd wonderkid, 19, promoted to England first-team training ahead of Uruguay clash
Football.content:
MANCHESTER UNITED youngster Ayden Heaven reportedly trained with England’s senior team ahead of friendlies against Uruguay and Japan.

The 19-year-old is being tipped for big things at Old Trafford after impressing since his move from Arsenal last year.





He’s already played 21 times for the Red Devils after just over a year in Manchester.

Heaven had been called up to the England Under-20s this week ahead of a clash against Italy on Friday.

But as Neil Ryan’s men continue their build-up, Heaven has joined the England senior side to help them prepare, according to Sky Sports.

Heaven was one of the players asked to support Thomas Tuchel‘s squad in the build-up to their first World Cup warm-up game.



The Three Lions play Uruguay on Friday before taking on Japan on Tuesday.

He is still expected to travel with the Under-20s for their trip to Italy this weekend.

Heaven will have linked up with United team-mates Harry Maguire, Kobbie Mainoo during his time with the squad at St George’s Park.

He has already started eight games in the Premier League this season after impressing Ruben Amorim earlier in the season.



The centre-back is yet to start a game under interim boss Michael Carrick.

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

But he’s come on as a sub four times, further adding to his experience after a solid spell under Amorim last season.

He played six times for United after the senior squad were hit by an injury crisis.

Heaven might have had more game time were it not for his own injury blow after suffering an ankle sprain last March that ruled him out for the rest of the 2024-25 season.



While Heaven will have a chance to impress in training with England, it is Mainoo who has a huge opportunity after earning a recall.

It’s the first time Mainoo has been selected since playing in England’s 2-1 Euro 2024 final defeat to Spain in summer 2024.

Mainoo has been one of the key figures in United’s resurgence under Carrick and he has started every game under him.

He will now be eager to win back his place in the England team for the upcoming 2026 World Cup.

Tuchel was open to the idea of Mainoo staking a claim for a first team spot after announcing his recall.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

The German said: “Kobbie is like Harry [Maguire]. A major part of the success story with Man United lately in the last [few] weeks.

“He plays all the time and I hear so many good things in the FA and from other players and I see his quality, so I want to meet him in person and see what he can do to get to know him better. Competition is on for him.”

Football.title: Arsenal ‘exploring transfer for PSG star Khvicha Kvaratskhelia’ with Mikel Arteta braced for hefty squad reshuffle
Football.content:
ARSENAL are reportedly exploring a stunning transfer move for Khvicha Kvaratskhelia.

The Paris Saint-Germain star has become one of the standout names in European football over the past four years.





Kvaratskhelia, 25, joined the French giants in January 2025 after an impressive three years with Napoli.

He helped the club win their first-ever Champions League title the same season and became the second Georgian to ever land the trophy.

The Independent has reported that the forward is now gaining the interest of Arsenal.

The report claims that the Gunners are planning a “considerable reshuffle” in the summer transfer window.



There is a desire to cash in on a senior player which would allow the North London club more “headroom” to cope with the Premier League’s Squad Cost Ratio.

It is also suggested that Kvaratskhelia’s camp is “open to the idea” of the switch.

The Georgian international scored three times across two legs against Chelsea in the Champions League last 16 tie.

The former Napoli man is thought to be capable of “elevating the level” of Arsenal’s squad.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET UP TO £100 BONUS

However, PSG’s Qatari owners are not keen on a sale of the player who is viewed as a prized asset.

Club sources are reported to have deemed Kvaratskhelia as “unsellable”.

This season has seen the star score 12 goals and assist seven more in 37 appearances in all competitions.

His performances have helped the club continue their dominance in Ligue 1.

PSG are currently top of the league table – one point ahead of RC Lens with a game in hand.



After dumping Chelsea out of the Champions League, PSG will take on Liverpool in the quarter-finals.

The first leg is set to take place at Anfield on April 14, giving Kvaratskhelia another chance to impress against Premier League opposition.

Football.title: Chelsea mulling over major stadium change with club considering abandoning ground
Football.content:
CHELSEA WOMEN are considering the possibility of moving all of their home games to Stamford Bridge from next season.

The Blues currently play the majority of their home fixtures at Kingsmeadow, AFC Wimbledon‘s former ground.





A select number of major WSL and European matches have been played at the Bridge this term, including the season opener against Manchester City and a London derby against Arsenal.

But the Cutback has claimed that from the 2026-27 campaign, the reigning league champions could move to the 40,000-seater stadium permanently.

The move would see them follow in the footsteps of rivals Arsenal, who began playing all of their WSL games at the Emirates Stadium this season.

It was revealed earlier this year that Chelsea were considering a move away from 4,850-seater Kingsmeadow.

The two London clubs faced off on Tuesday night in the Uefa Women’s Champions League.

The Gunners came out on top with a 3-1 win at the Emirates to take a commanding position in the two-legged tie.

Head coach Sonia Bompastor said: “It’s important for clubs like Chelsea to have a back-up plan.

“The club is having some really good conversations about the future and the stadium because when you play many games in a season with your men’s team and women’s team, and you play Champions League, sometimes you can have clashes.”



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET UP TO £100 BONUS

Chelsea currently sit third in the WSL table, nine points behind leaders Manchester City.

They next face Aston Villa on Sunday before hosting Arsenal at the Bridge next Wednesday.

Football.title: Man Utd in further new stadium blow as FA snub 100,000-seater ‘Wembley of the North’ for major event
Football.content:
THE FA will snub Manchester United’s new stadium and assign Wembley to host the 2035 Women’s World Cup final.

United have pledged to build a ‘Wembley of the North’ featuring a 100,000 capacity and state-of-the-art facilities.





But it’s the Wembley of North London which is set to get the nod.

The UK has no rival bidders for the tournament, with final proposals due to be sent to Fifa by Friday.

And FA chief Mark Bullingham has revealed the near-certain host venue for the World Cup’s showpiece fixture.

He told Sky News: “It’s a Fifa decision and our full expectation is Wembley will host the final.



“We obviously want some of the new stadiums that are being built and Old Trafford would be one of those.

“But we also think we’ve got some existing settings that are really strong as well.

“So with Wembley hosting the final, and those other ones supporting us through the tournament, that’ll be a great combination.”

Plans for the new United stadium were announced in March but there is still uncertainty around who will fund the £2billion build.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

The Red Devils were reportedly interested in hosting the 2035 Women’s World Cup final and want to complete construction well before the tournament.

But the five-year timescale announced by co-owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe only starts when United are cleared to start building.

Chief of United’s New Stadium Development, Collette Roche, told MUTV: “It does take one or two years to get ready for construction; to get the land assembled, to get the funds in place and to get the planning permission.



“Andy Burnham, the mayor [of Manchester], said his ambition would be for us to host the final for the Women’s World Cup in 2035, so if we could pull that off, that would be incredible.”

The Women’s World Cup has never been staged in the UK, and Wembley has not hosted a World Cup final since England’s men won there in 1966.

Football.title: ITV rule out using controversial broadcast feature at World Cup after Six Nations backlash
Football.content:
ITV have ruled out using a controversial feature during this summer’s World Cup broadcasts.

The broadcaster will have a share of the matches from the tournament being hosted across the US, Canada and Mexico.



ITV will share the responsibility of showing England‘s progress throughout the 2026 World Cup with the BBC.



However, one feature that appeared during the Six Nations will not be making a return.

Fans were left fuming during the rugby union tournament after ITV used “picture in picture” to display an advert during matches.

Fifa have allowed the practice to be used by broadcasters for the World Cup.

The 104 matches at the tournament will all have three-minute drinks breaks during each half.

The organisation does restrict adverts to only its own commercial partners and tournament sponsors.

ITV would also be monitored by regulator Ofcom as it can only display adverts for an allotted amount of time.

Therefore, by showing adverts in the drinks break, a later segment for adverts would have to be reduced.

SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET UP TO £100 BONUS

Taking all of this into account, ITV have decided against the practice for the World Cup.



The Times has reported that there was a “huge amount of interest” in the broadcaster’s advertising packages.

The “picture in picture” ads at the Six Nations were seen as a success but there is “less appeal” in using them at the World Cup.

The Three Lions will kickstart their attempt to end 60-years or hurt against Croatia in Group L.

Thomas Tuchel‘s side will then have games against Ghana and Panama as they look to progress to the knockouts.

Football.title: Fans baffled by ‘by far the weirdest thing ever seen on the stage’ as darts star brings bizarre item to the oche
Football.content:
DARTS fans have been left baffled after a darts star brought a CHEESE SANDWICH onto the stage.

Players have got up to all sorts of strange antics down the years.





But few can top what happened at Modus Super Series 13, Week 10.

Darts star Richie Howson brought a cheese sandwich with him on stage in a packed lunch as he faced Zereso Zeverboom.

A shocked commentator exclaimed: “That’s a sandwich!”

His co-commentator added: “Well, we said he’s a plain sort of man, just a cheese filling there.”



Fans were equally surprised by the presence of the sarnie on stage.

One reacted saying: “He’s Richie Howson and he can do what he wants. 🦉”

Another added: “Only Richie could think a cheese sandwich is the secret weapon for a World Championship. What’s next, a pint of milk for the double?”

A third wrote: “Man’s gotta eat. 😋”



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

And another commented: “One hundred and atety. I’ll get my coat. 🧥

A fifth remarked: “Everyone needs a mascot.”

Football.title: Cult football club demands to be RELEGATED regardless of final league table as FA request submitted
Football.content:
HASHTAG UNITED have filed an official request to be RELEGATED – even if they finish above the drop zone.

The Essex club are well-known for their massive presence on YouTube where they have racked up almost 700,000 subscribers.





Hashtag only played exhibition matches until they entered into the Eastern Counties Division 1 South in 2018.

But three promotions in seven seasons catapulted them into the Isthmian League Premier Division – the seventh tier on the English football pyramid – which demands higher spending on things like away game travel, stadium infrastructure and medical requirements.

League bosses have now confirmed that Hashtag have asked to be relegated despite the fact they currently sit 18th – one place and one point above the bottom four.

Hashtag have now issued their own statement and will get their way pending FA approval.



The team that finishes fourth from bottom will likely be spared come the end of the campaign.

Hashtag founder Spencer Owen said in a lengthy statement: “Last Thursday, on our actual tenth anniversary, I sat in a room with our players and told them something difficult.

“We have requested that our men’s team step down from the Isthmian Premier Division at the end of the 2025/26 season.

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

“We are choosing this because, after a long time thinking, we believe it gives us the best possible foundation for what comes next.



“I think the game is broken. It’s not football that’s become God. It’s money. Football’s governance is lacking. The finances are unsustainable at almost every level.

“We want to have the best players we can and play at the highest level possible, but not at the cost of our staff’s jobs and not at the cost of us being able to continue our mission of innovating and changing the game.

“So many teams burn out as a result of flying too close to the sun. We have to stand for something bigger than just winning.

“Moving to Step 4 will mean significant budgetary savings that will be reinvested into other key parts of the business at a crucial time.

“This could be the best thing that ever happened to us, at the perfect time. This is not a relegation. It is a revolution.”



Famous online and TV personalities Theo Baker, Toby Aromolaran and PK Humble are among those to have turned out for Hashtag United.

The club also counts Chelsea legend Cesar Azpilicueta as a co-owner and board member.

Football.title: Man Utd to go abroad for training camp after Michael Carrick given 24 DAYS without a match
Football.content:
MANCHESTER UNITED will head to the Republic of Ireland for a four-day training camp next month.

The visit is also expected to coincide with the announcement of a pre-season friendly in Dublin.





United are due to fly to Ireland on April 6 and spend three nights at their training base.

Sources say United will use the time in Ireland for an intense training period and extended time together to prepare for their final seven fixtures.

Sun Sport revealed United are expected to play five friendlies in Helsinki, Gothenburg, Oslo, Warsaw and Dublin ahead of next season.

United will go 24 days without playing by the time they host Leeds United on April 13.



United are out of the FA Cup and have no European football this season, while the Leeds game has been picked by Sky Sports for their Monday Night Football slot.

The Ireland jaunt will mark United’s only European trip in a season where they will play only 40 matches – their lowest since 1914-15.

Michael Carrick’s squad will be based around 30 minutes outside Dublin.

In the absence of a mid-season tour to Saudi Arabia, United have decided to engage in some commercial work around the Ireland training camp.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

United seemed set to head to Saudi in the absence of any European football this season but eventually decided against the jaunt amid their resurgence under Carrick.

Dublin hosted United friendlies in 2017 and 2023 when they faced Sampdoria and Athletic Bilbao.

Football.title: ITV forced into 2026 World Cup U-turn weeks after massive backlash over in-game ads
Football.content:
ITV forced into 2026 World Cup U-turn weeks after massive backlash over in-game ads

ITV has decided against using the controversial feature for this summer's World Cup after the broadcaster faced a significant backlash when it was rolled out for the Six Nations

View 2 Images ITV have decided to ditch the in‑game ads for the World Cup

ITV have ruled out using split‑screen adverts during the 2026 World Cup after the backlash to in‑game ads shown during the Six Nations. FIFA confirmed in December that each half will include a single three‑minute stoppage, creating the opportunity for broadcasters to run adverts while play is paused for the first time.

‌



Previously, ads were only allowed at half‑time. During the tournament, all 104 matches will include three‑minute drinks breaks in each half.

‌



FIFA has instructed broadcasters that these pauses cannot begin within 20 seconds of the referee stopping play, and coverage must return to the match feed at least 30 seconds before play resumes. However, according to the Daily Mail, ITV will not be taking up the option.

‌



The broadcaster, monitored by Ofcom, is said to be restricted by the total amount of advertising it is permitted to show, meaning split‑screen ads would force reductions in traditional commercial breaks. FIFA also limits the breaks to its own commercial partners and tournament sponsors.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

FIFA 'pressing' Donald Trump to make exception as huge World Cup issue laid bare READ MORE :

UK broadcasters have traditionally shown live sport without interruptions, only cutting to adverts at half-time or between periods of play. That changed during the Six Nations, when split‑screen ads were introduced.

‌



The move didn't go down well with rugby fans at the time. One viewer wrote at the time: "Watching rugby on ITV - what's this split‑screen advertising about? So irritating. Leave it till half-time."

Another added: "Shocking split of the screen during the rugby for an advert!" A third said: "Hating this advert split‑screen during the rugby."

View 2 Images The World Cup is set to take place in the USA, Canada and Mexico this summer (Image: Getty Images)

‌



The World Cup arrives in the United States, Canada and Mexico in June, with the opening match now less than three months away on Thursday, June 11. Across the three host nations, 16 stadiums will stage games - 11 in the USA, three in Mexico and two in Canada.

ITV will have the responsibility of showing England’s games throughout the tournament alongside the BBC.

Football.title: Mo Salah 'already tempted' by £200m mega-offer after Liverpool exit as one move ruled out
Football.content:
Mo Salah 'already tempted' by £200m mega-offer after Liverpool exit as one move ruled out

Liverpool and Mohamed Salah will part ways in the summer and there has already been some clarity on his next move with Saudi and MLS transfers having been touted

View 2 Images Mohamed Salah has two possible Liverpool exit routes that he seems to have very different opinions on (Image: (Photo by Michael Regan/Getty Images))

Mohamed Salah is said to have already made a definitive decision on what his next move won't be after announcing his Liverpool exit. The Egypt international confirmed his departure for the end of the season on Tuesday and it has been reported by The Sun that he is not interested in following in Lionel Messi's footsteps with a transfer to the MLS.

‌



They have claimed however, that the 33-year-old is tempted to become the Saudi Pro League’s new poster boy in a sensational £200million-plus offer. There has been a serious interest from the Middle East in Salah for a number of years.

‌



Al Ittihad are considered the most realistic destination having tried to bring in Salah in a blockbuster transfer just under three years ago with Liverpool turning down a £150million bid. However, Al-Qadsiah, managed by former Liverpool boss Brendan Rodgers, have reportedly pulled out of the race.

‌



Salah remaining in Europe is another possibility if he is open to that, as per The Sun, with clubs La Liga and Serie A clubs expected to be interested. Before joining Liverpool, Salah had one extremely successful season in Italy at AS Roma.

Liverpool encouraged to seal 'unbelievable' transfer as Mo Salah replacement READ MORE :

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

While so much has been said on Salah's next move, there has also been plenty of verdicts delivered on who will replace him at Anfield. Liverpool icon Steven Gerrard has recently made his thoughts perfectly clear on the attributes required before then naming the winger he wants to see come in.

‌



He told TNT Sports: "You have to buy someone who is box-office or you're not replacing him. They have to be top-end, top three (or) four wingers in the world to replace Mo Salah because that’s what Mo has been.

“If you are Liverpool, you have got to go ready. Tried and tested. For me that would be [Michael] Olise. Somebody of that calibre.

View 2 Images Liverpool have been told to sign Michael Olise as their Mohamed Salah replacement

‌



“If you don’t ask, you don’t get. And I wouldn’t want to see him leave Bayern because that front three is amazing. But if you are talking (about) Olise on that right-hand side for Liverpool, that is box office.”

The former Crystal Palace star is one of many marquee options that have been linked over the last 24 hours with Manchester United-linked Yan Diomande as another. Desire Doue is another name that fits the requirements set by Gerrard with Paris Saint-Germain teammate Khvicha Kvaratskhelia another explosive attacking option.

Arsenal have since emerged as contenders for his signature this summer as they look for a marquee left-sided forward. The Georgian can play in every position across the front three, though.

Football.title: Khvicha Kvaratskhelia 'open to Arsenal move' as PSG star's camp leak transfer stance
Football.content:
Khvicha Kvaratskhelia 'open to Arsenal move' as PSG star's camp leak transfer stance

Mikel Arteta's Arsenal are eyeing a possible transfer for Paris Saint-Germain star Khvicha Kvaratskhelia, with a summer move not ruled out by the winger's camp

View 2 Images Arsenal are looking at signing Paris Saint-Germain star Khvicha Kvaratskhelia in the summer transfer window (Image: (Photo by Catherine Steenkeste/Getty Images))

Arsenal are exploring the possibility of a summer transfer for Paris Saint-Germain star Khvicha Kvaratskhelia.

‌



According to The Independent, the Gunners are expected to undergo a significant squad overhaul this season, with Kvaratskhelia emerging as a key target.

‌



The 25-year-old only moved to PSG in January 2024 for £59million, but it's now reported that his camp would actually be open to a move.

‌



His current club, however, is unlikely to share this view, given his crucial role and the fact that the winger is seen as the gem of their ongoing project.

Meanwhile, in north London, the sale of a senior player is being seriously considered, with Arsenal aiming to sanction sales this summer. This would provide the club with more flexibility in light of the Premier League's change in financial regulations to Squad Cost Ratio.

Despite this, Arsenal seem set to splash out this summer, with Kvaratskhelia an offensive addition that would instantly elevate Mikel Arteta's squad.

‌



However, a deal seems highly complicated with the Georgian still having three years left on his contract. Although his representatives haven't ruled out a transfer, PSG are unlikely to consider a departure unless substantial funds are offered.

Luis Enrique's team knocked out Arsenal on their journey to clinching the Champions League last season, with both clubs in the quarter-finals this time around, and the two sides could potentially face off in the final.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



The reigning champions thrashed Chelsea 8-2 on aggregate earlier this month, with Kvaratskhelia finding the back of the net three times. That accounts for a quarter of his goals this season, with the versatile forward also providing seven assists from a total of 37 appearances.

However, it might require some significant persuasion to convince Kvaratskhelia to make the switch to the Premier League, given his recent remarks about PSG.

He told Le Parisien: "Paris, for me, is truly incredible. You have everything here, and on top of that, it's the city of love. When PSG called me, I think my wife was the happiest person in the world.

‌



"Before PSG contacted me, she always said, 'Imagine if we played for PSG one day, it would be incredible to live there.' I would reply, 'Yes, of course.'

View 2 Images Khvicha Kvaratskhelia scored against Chelsea in the Champions League earlier this month (Image: Getty Images)

"But in my mind, I thought it was very difficult to go there when there were such amazing players like [Lionel] Messi, Neymar and [Kylian] Mbappe.

Article continues below

"I love Paris, I love everything about Paris. The more I think about it, the more I appreciate how respectful people are. When you go out, they don't bother you much. At a restaurant, for example, they ask before taking a photo. I really like that. It's the best city to stroll around in with your wife."

Atletico Madrid's Julian Alvarez has also been suggested as a genuine target for the Gunners, with the former Manchester City forward flourishing in Spain.

Football.title: Benfica handed punishment by UEFA after racism incident during Real Madrid clash
Football.content:
Benfica handed punishment by UEFA after racism incident during Real Madrid clash

UEFA have confirmed that Portuguese giants Benfica have been hit with a fine and a suspended stadium closure after a racist incident during their Champions League showdown with Real Madrid

View 2 Images Vinicius Jr during Real Madrid's clash with Benfica (Image: Getty Images)

Benfica have been hit with a fine and suspended stadium closure after supporters were accused of racist behaviour against Real Madrid. The Portuguese giants took on the Spanish side in the Champions League playoff round last month.

‌



The clash was marred by a number of unsavoury incidents including Brazilian winger Vinicius Jr accusing Gianluca Prestianni of using racist language. An investigation was launched in the aftermath.

‌



And UEFA have now confirmed that Benfica will face punishment for the conduct of their fans, with racist behaviour having been identified. A statement from the European governing body read that Benfica had been accused of: “Throwing of objects, use of laser pointer, Racist and/or discriminatory behaviour (illicit chants and gestures by two supporters), unsporting conduct by assistant coach Pedro Luis Ferreira Machado.”

‌



On the punishment that Benfica will face, the statement added: “To fine SL Benfica €40,000 and to order the partial closure of SL Benfica’s stadium (i.e. 500 seats in sectors 10 and/or 11), during the next one (1) UEFA club competition match in which SL Benfica will play as host club, for the racist and/or discriminatory behaviour of its supporters. Said stadium closure is suspended during a probationary period of one (1) year, starting from the date of the present decision."

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Real Madrid take immediate action after Kylian Mbappe scan on WRONG knee READ MORE :

Meanwhile, the club have also been fined €8,000 for use of laser pointer and a further €25,000 for the throwing of objects. Finally, assistant coach Machado has been suspended for one game.

‌



Benfica eventually ended up going out of the Champions League against Real Madrid, having lost 3-1 on aggregate. The clash was however marred by an incident between Vinicius and Prestianni at the Estadio Da Luz, while Benfica boss Jose Mourinho was also criticised for his response.

Speaking at the time, the former Manchester United and Chelsea boss said: "I told him [Vinicius], when you score a goal like that you just celebrate and walk back. They [Vinicius Jr and Prestianni] told me different things. But I don't believe in one or another. I want to be an independent.

View 2 Images Vinicius Jr accused Gianluca Prestianni of racist language (Image: Getty Images)

‌



"When he was arguing about racism, I told him the biggest person [Eusebio] in the history of this club was black. This club, the last thing that it is, is racist."

It was reported that Prestianni had admitted to his teammates that he had used a racial slur during the game, something that Benfica strongly denied. A statement read: "Sport Lisboa e Benfica categorically denies that the player Prestianni has communicated to the squad or the club's structure that he has uttered a racist insult to the player Vinicius Jr, from Real Madrid.

"As has already been made public, the player apologised to his teammates for the incident that occurred during the match against Real Madrid, regretting the dimension and consequences of it and assuring everyone, as he has done since the first hour, that he is not racist."

Football.title: Ange Postecoglou offers Spurs prediction amid relegation fears - ‘Not been easy’
Football.content:
Ange Postecoglou offers Spurs prediction amid relegation fears - ‘Not been easy’

Tottenham Hotspur are deep in the relegation mire after a disastrous Premier League campaign and former boss Ange Postecoglou has shared his verdict on them staying up

View 2 Images Ange Postecoglou has shared his verdict on Spurs' woes (Image: Getty Images)

Ange Postecoglou still believes Tottenham can avoid relegation from the Premier League, having admitted their woes have not been easy to watch. The North London side have endured a miserable season this term.

‌



Following the weekend’s shocking 3-0 defeat at the hands of relegation rivals Nottingham Forest, Spurs sit 17th and just one point clear of West Ham United with seven games to be played.

‌



Postecoglou, who was sacked by Spurs in the summer after helping them to the Europa League, believes that there is still enough talent within the squad for them to avoid a place in the Championship.

‌



Speaking to Australian broadcaster SEN 1116, he said: “I still have a really strong attachment to the club. It was two years and they were by no means easy years, but we invested a lot into them.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Man Utd change their mind on Roberto De Zerbi as Tottenham target's next job becomes clear READ MORE :

Mauricio Pochettino makes admission over Premier League return after Tottenham talks emerge READ MORE :

“Second last game we won a European trophy, which was an incredible high. The connection there will be there forever. To watch them struggle has not been easy and it’s not the way I thought it would go.

‌



“They’re in a hell of a fight, relegation is massive for any club but for Tottenham, it’s a pretty big deal. They have some fighting to do and they have the quality to get out. They need a circuit-breaker, for sure.”

Two different faces have taken the reins at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium this season - Thomas Frank having been replaced by Igor Tudor. Postecoglou has also had a dramatic season, having been sacked by Nottingham Forest after just eight games.

Asked what he is looking for in his next job, the Australian added: “I’ve figured out that for me to do what I do, the start of pre-season is a good starting point, Jumping in mid-season – especially in the Premier League but in most leagues – is just probably too much of a challenge for the way I do things. That’s what I’m holding out for.

‌



View 2 Images Postecoglou helped Spurs to the Europa League title (Image: Getty Images)

“Where that is and what it looks like is hard to say at the moment, but I haven’t even scratched the surface of what I want to do over here yet and the impact I want to make, the football I want to play.

“I want to win things still. I still have that drive and passion to achieve, that hasn’t changed. The fact now it’s the first time I’m coming off a bad experience – that’s just more fuel for me.

Article continues below

“Wherever I go in there will be plenty of scepticism – that’s brilliant, that’s what I need. Get the gloves, put the helmet on again on and go really hard.”

Football.title: Liverpool encouraged to seal 'unbelievable' transfer as Mo Salah replacement
Football.content:
Liverpool encouraged to seal 'unbelievable' transfer as Mo Salah replacement

Alan Shearer has backed Liverpool to replace Mohamed Salah with an 'unbelievable' player after the 33-year-old announced he will be leaving the club this summer

View 2 Images Liverpool have been backed to replace Mohamed Salah with another world-class player (Image: (Photo by Liverpool FC/Liverpool FC via Getty Images))

Alan Shearer has thrown his support behind Liverpool to replace Mohamed Salah with an "unbelievable" player.

‌



The 33-year-old dropped a bombshell exit video announcing he will be departing the club this summer, with Shearer being the latest ex-footballer to voice his reaction.

‌



He told Betfair: "I guess it was always going to be a difficult decision for him to leave Liverpool because he was given a two-year contract last year. He's got one year left and obviously he could sit on that.

‌



"But that wouldn't be the right thing to do so it's been mutually agreed after all he's done for the club and achieved and what the club's done for him and the platform that they've given him so yeah, they've come to the right decision for both parties I think.

Liverpool's scary front three next season with Mo Salah funds spent on two signings READ MORE :

"I've always said it's better to get off the stage when people are shouting for more and it's the right time for him. He's a great, there's no doubt about it, so it is the right time, yeah.

"He is a big gap to fill now for Liverpool, an unbelievable gap. But so was Kevin Keegan, John Toshack, Kenny Dalglish, Ian Rush, Peter Beardsley, Alan Hansen and Steven Gerrard.

‌



"That football club somehow has some unbelievable greats, but they always go out and somehow find another one. I mean, good luck trying to replace him, that's going to be a difficult task.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

"Liverpool Football Club always do that. They've got the young boy, Rio Ngumoha so they may give him more opportunities. But I guess they'll have to go out and get someone."

‌



Precisely who Liverpool will target in the transfer market remains uncertain, but there are pressing issues that Arne Slot needs to address first.

The reigning champions have looked shaky at the back on numerous occasions this campaign, with Shearer delivering a clear verdict.

The former Newcastle United forward said: "Liverpool's inconsistency has been ridiculous, but most teams have been like that in the Premier League, other than perhaps Arsenal. With Liverpool, I guess the spotlight is bigger because of how much they've spent and what they've spent it on.

‌



View 2 Images Liverpool have won just two of their last six games in all competitions (Image: CameraSport via Getty Images)

"They look as if they're on their last legs. I was a little bit reluctant to go overboard after the result against Galatasaray at Anfield because of the weak opposition.

"They've gone into that game in the Premier League and it's back to having no energy, back to being all over the place. I mean, Van Dijk, Konate and the back four were a mess. They were all over the place and Brighton deserved to win the game. It was really, really poor from Liverpool again.

Article continues below

"They look very, very tired. They don't look cohesive in terms of how easy it is for teams to get through them. Whereas certainly for large parts of last season, they had energy, they looked solid defensively and they're totally opposite to that now.

"But they're still in the Champions League and FA Cup and they're still going for Champions League football in terms of the top four or top five. But what we've seen up to now, particularly league-wise has been miles off what Liverpool need."

Football.title: Man City 115 charges timeline verdicts, '60-point deduction' response, punishment hint
Football.content:
Man City 115 charges timeline verdicts, '60-point deduction' response, punishment hint

Manchester City's ongoing Premier League financial charges case continues with speculation over verdict timings and potential punishments

View 2 Images Manchester City are still waiting for the outcome of their financial charges case against the Premier League (Image: (Photo by Serena Taylor/Newcastle United via Getty Images))

The financial charges case against Manchester City by the Premier League is still in progress. There have been conflicting reports recently about when a decision might be reached, with some suggesting the Easter period or summer, while others report that there's no update on a potential timeline.

‌



Depending on the verdict of the case, a range of penalties have been proposed, with the most recent suggestion being a 60-point deduction. City got a taste of possible repercussions when Chelsea were slapped with a £10million fine even after co-operating with proceedings.

‌



Nearly a year and a half ago, a 12-week hearing between City lawyers and the Premier League took place, with everyone in football still awaiting the final outcome. City were accused of 115 breaches of financial rules, though the actual number is thought to be closer to 130. The club strongly denies any wrongdoing.

‌



OPINION John Cross Pep Guardiola drops biggest hint on Man City future yet with Carabao Cup final action

Marc Guehi's reaction to being denied Man City Carabao Cup final medal says it all READ MORE :

With all this in mind, Mirror Sport has delved deeper into the latest updates on City's financial charges case, including a timeline, hint at punishment, and the shocking claim of points deduction.

Timeline verdicts

In mid-March, the Athletic reported that unnamed sources from both parties in the case had received no updates from the independent panel regarding timescales.

‌



However, The Independent's Miguel Delaney disclosed earlier this week, via the podcast 'Libero', that his latest information suggested the outcome would emerge over Easter, but it has now reverted to being sometime during the summer.

FOLLOW OUR MAN CITY FB PAGE! Follow our Manchester City Mirror Football Facebook page

Chelsea's case concerning alleged illicit payments has diverted attention away from City in recent weeks with the club's new ownership voluntarily co-operating with UEFA for a resolution. Even so, it still required three years for a verdict to materialise.

‌



Chelsea's circumstances were also discussed on the Libero podcast with Delaney revealing that "multiple executives from the Premier League are saying that there is potential legal precedence from this case that City's lawyers will be licking their lips at".

'60-point deduction' claim

Former City defender Richard Dunne has offered a clear response to speculation that City face a 60-point deduction. He said, via Goal: "It's been ridiculous already, the amount of time that it's dragged on. They've come to a conclusion a long time ago, so why it's taken so long to get around to giving out their results of what they found is stupid.

"I mean, it just undermines the whole Premier League if they start coming out now at this stage of the season and you've got the opportunity of a real interesting title race over the next couple of months.

‌



"So, they're ruining their own competition if they come out and start making decisions now. It's something that's best left until the summer and they can work out what they're going to do going forward rather than retrospectively."

He added: "I can imagine for Manchester City it's been really undermining because it's just been dragging along with them. It's followed them around and for myself and for other ex-players, or anyone that's associated with the club, you do interviews and it's always about, 'but what about this FFP thing?'.

View 2 Images Nico O'Reilly scored twice to help Manchester City win the Carabao Cup (Image: Simon Dael/Shutterstock)

‌



"Man City have got so much going for them in terms of they just won the Carabao Cup, they constantly produce really good players that play a really good style of football and everyone wants to just talk about the negative side of it.

"So, I think for the whole thing to get dealt with and to get finished, I think it's a relief for everybody from Man City.

"I think it'll be a relief for the Premier League that they can finally come to a decision and put the whole thing to bed and let Man City be what they are and let them deal with whatever comes their way and get back to just focusing purely on the football."

‌



Punishment hint

As disclosed by the Premier League, Chelsea's co-operation aided their case in determining the penalty for financial infringements.

Part of their statement read: "When considering the appropriate sanction, the Premier League Board noted that the club's proactive self-reporting, admissions of breach and exceptional cooperation throughout the investigation acted as significant mitigating factors."

This is alleged to be in stark contrast to City, with the Premier League accusing them of failing to 'cooperate with, and assist, the Premier League in its investigations, including by providing documents and information to the Premier League in the utmost good faith'.

Article continues below

This could imply that, if found guilty, City might not be shown the same leniency that was extended to Chelsea.

Football.title: Luke Littler hosts darts tournament for England stars at St George's Park
Football.content:
Luke Littler hosts darts tournament for England stars at St George's Park

Thomas Tuchel’s England are taking part in their final camp before attention turns to the World Cup and the Three Lions have been given a darts masterclass by Luke Littler

View 2 Images Luke Littler poses with England's Jude Bellingham (Image: Getty Images)

Darts star Luke Littler linked up with the England squad at St George’s Park for a pre-World Cup masterclass. The reigning darts world champion travelled to the Three Lions’ base this week and lent his support to the side.

‌



Thomas Tuchel’s England are set to take on Uruguay and Japan over the coming week in friendly showdowns at Wembley. And while the German will be keen for victory, some stars already have bragging rights, having claimed the title in an inter-camp darts tournament.

‌



Manchester City star Phil Foden took on Newcastle goalkeeper Aaron Ramsdale in the final, with the latter claiming the crown despite the former having hit an eye-catching 180.

‌



Each of the squad donned their own Littler-themed shirts, with a swap having also taken place. Littler also presented the squad with a personalised darts board emblazoned with the Three Lions and a ‘Good Luck’ message.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

After the link-up, Littler said: “It was a dream as a football fan to come here to see the facilities, meet all the players and sit down and have dinner and have a laugh with them. We played a bit of darts - as they were warming up, Thomas Tuchel told me to teach these boys a lesson!

‌



“There’s some good potential there in Phil Foden and Aaron Ramsdale. James Trafford had a little bit as well. The others not so much! I wished the boys luck this summer - lets hope they can bring it home.”

He also shared his take on the visit on his Instagram page alongside a number of photos. Littler wrote: “None of the @england lads giving me a run for my money just yet. Quality day thanks for having me. Bring on the summer.”

View 2 Images Littler linked up with the England squad at St George's Park (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Meanwhile, Ramsdale relished the chance to go head-to-head with Littler. He added: “It was great to have Luke visit St. George's Park to meet the squad and wish us well. Personally, it was an honour for me to have the chance to step up to the oche and take on the world champion.”

England will now turn their attentions to their upcoming commitments at Wembley, with Friday evening’s clash with Uruguay set to come. Tuchel’s side will then host Japan on Tuesday.

England have been drawn to face Croatia, Ghana and Panama in Group L of this summer’s World Cup.
